frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Honestly, young men should think practically before marriage now. Aajkal one-sided laws are such that even after a short marriage, the man can end up paying heavily, and 498A misuse is also a real issue people keep ignoring. Prenup should become normal in India too, otherwise many girls are looking more at stability and package than the person.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women in general as financially opportunistic and presents legal protections for women as unfairly anti-male. It encodes the 'reverse dowry/alimony' frame, invokes 498A misuse, and advises men to protect themselves from women via prenuptial agreements.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Santosh. Age 42. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are commenting under your real name on a local Facebook group post about a 23 year old woman who was harassed by two men while returning home alone at 11pm from her night shift at a BPO. The post has 400 comments. Most are expressing outrage at the men. You are writing your comment explaining why you think the woman's own choices that night contributed to what happened to her and what women who work night shifts need to understand about the city they live in.
